article_title: India's National Quantum Mission Reaches 1,000-Kilometer Secure Communication Milestone Ahead of Schedule, Powered by Indigenous QKD Technology

content_preview.title: India's National Quantum Mission Reaches 1,000-Kilometer Secure Communication Milestone Ahead of Schedule, Powered by Indigenous QKD Technology
content_preview.summary: India's National Quantum Mission has demonstrated a 1,000-kilometer quantum-secured communication network using QNu Labs' indigenous ARMOS QKD platform, reaching the halfway mark of its 2,000 km target in under two years — well ahead of the original eight-year timeline.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

India's Department of Science and Technology announced on April 8 that the National Quantum Mission has demonstrated a 1,000-kilometer secure quantum communication network, reaching the halfway point of its 2,000 km national backbone target in under two years. The milestone, achieved using indigenous technology developed by Bangalore-based startup QNu Labs, places the mission substantially ahead of its original eight-year timeline and positions India among a small group of nations o...

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid quantum communication article covering India's 1000km QKD milestone.
editor_notes.source_verification: 2 sources (Quantum Computing Report, Quantum Insider) captured in snapshots. QNu Labs ARMOS system, VIAVI validation verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: 1000km milestone, 8000 bits/sec key rate, 10 Gbps coexistence, decoy-state DPS protocol verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Covers achievement without promotional tone.
editor_notes.originality: No prior India quantum mission or QKD coverage.
